steps.title: Identify the disparity in seed dispersal
steps.content: The passage states bulbuls disperse more seeds from non-native plants than from native plants at risk, because non-natives are more abundant.

steps.title: Apply the population consequence
steps.content:
More seeds dispersed from non-native plants means faster population growth for those plants. Choice A states this outcome directly: non-native plants increase in population faster than vulnerable native plants. Choices B, C, and D introduce information not discussed in the passage—when the non-native shift began, where natives currently grow—or contradict it (night-blooming jessamine is thriving, not endangered).
That matches choice A — A is correct.
